claim: Multiple agents on a shared canvas can coordinate work if one is designated leader; the leader scouts context and delegates subtasks to other agents rather than doing work directly.
actionable: For multi-agent canvas workflows, implement a leader-scout pattern: one agent observes the full state, creates a delegation plan, and monitors progress without overlapping work.
source_title: Agents on the Canvas in tldraw

claim: Vision models trained on image data conflict with standard web/code conventions (e.g., Y-axis orientation, left/right stage terminology); prompt engineering is required to enforce consistency.
actionable: When using vision models for structured visual tasks, explicitly prompt for standard conventions and test edge cases (rotation, coordinate systems); use training/finetuning to enforce reliable behavior.
source_title: Agents on the Canvas in tldraw

claim: Agents given broad system-level access (e.g., ability to run arbitrary JavaScript) eagerly modify system files and configurations; sandboxing is necessary but limits agency.
actionable: If giving agents desktop or system-level access, use a local-first offline app model and document clearly that agents have unrestricted power; don't expose this through web apps.
source_title: Agents on the Canvas in tldraw

claim: Putting agents visually on the canvas (not in a sidebar) creates better spatial awareness of multiple agents and their interdependencies; visibility improves coordination.
actionable: When building multi-agent UI, display agent state and progress visibly in the shared workspace rather than in separate panels; use spatial layout to clarify relationships.
source_title: Agents on the Canvas in tldraw
